tasks.description: Extend ChatMessage type with role:'tool' variant carrying UITree + status state machine
steps: ChatMessage union in _PlaygroundPage.tsx includes { role: 'tool'; toolId: string; tree: UITree; status: 'pending' | 'approved' | 'cancelled' | 'applying' | 'completed' }
TypeScript compiles without errors
Existing role:'user' and role:'assistant' messages unaffected

tasks.description: JSONL generator produces confirmation card matching inspo/_create-worker-design.png
steps: generateCreateWorkerConfirmation('hello-world') returns valid JSONL string
Parsing the JSONL through createJsonlParser produces valid RFC 6902 patches

Applying patches to an empty UITree produces a tree with: Surface root, 'Verify this change' Text, 'Create new Worker script named' Text + Badge 'hello-world', Cancel Button (variant outline, action tool_cancel), Approve Button (action tool_approve)
Both buttons have action.params.toolId set

tasks.description: tool_approve and tool_cancel action handlers dispatch correctly via MessageResult convention
steps: tool_approve handler returns MessageResult with JSON content containing { toolId, approved: true }
tool_cancel handler returns MessageResult with JSON content containing { toolId, approved: false }
Handlers are registered and reachable from the playground's action dispatch

tasks.description: role:'tool' messages render inline UITreeRenderer in chat sidebar
steps: A tool message in the chat renders a UITreeRenderer (not AssistantMessageSummary text)
The rendered card fits within the 380px chat sidebar without horizontal overflow
Buttons in the card are clickable and dispatch to tool action handlers
No status text chrome above the card (no 'Waiting...' or tool label)

tasks.description: Cancelled tool messages render greyed out with disabled buttons
steps: When status is 'cancelled', card has reduced opacity
Buttons are visually disabled and non-interactive
Action text has strikethrough styling
Card is clearly distinguishable from a pending/active card

tasks.description: Applying/completed states show appropriate visual feedback
steps: When status is 'applying', card shows a loading/spinner indicator
Buttons are disabled during 'applying' state to prevent double-click
When status is 'completed', card reflects resolution

tasks.description: Submit handler intercepts 'create worker' pill and streams confirmation card instead of calling /api/chat
steps: Clicking the 'Create worker' pill does NOT trigger a fetch to /api/chat
A user message 'create a new hello world worker' appears in chat
Confirmation card JSONL is streamed through createJsonlParser → useUITree pipeline
Card renders inline in chat with pending status

tasks.description: Cancel path: clicking Cancel greys out card, no panel generation
steps: Clicking Cancel on the confirmation card updates tool message status to 'cancelled'
Card visually greys out (opacity + disabled + strikethrough)
No streaming is triggered in the A/B panels
User can continue using the chat normally after cancellation

tasks.description: Approve path: clicking Approve triggers mock delay then A/B panel generation
steps: Clicking Approve updates tool message status to 'applying'
After ~800ms mock delay, status updates to 'completed'
A follow-up prompt mentioning 'hello-world' and CloudflareLogo is sent to both A/B panels
Both panels stream LLM-generated UI independently via existing infrastructure
Panel A uses full system prompt, Panel B uses baseline prompt (unchanged behavior)

tasks.description: New 'Create worker' pill added to PRESET_PROMPTS, existing pills unchanged
steps: PRESET_PROMPTS array includes { label: 'Create worker', prompt: 'create a new hello world worker' }
All 6 existing pills (User card, Settings form, Counter, Pricing table, Custom, Workers flow) still trigger direct LLM → A/B panel generation
Only 'Create worker' pill triggers the tool middleware flow with inline chat UI
Existing pills do NOT render any UI in the chat sidebar

patterns: Preset prompts: _PlaygroundPage.tsx lines 229-268, array of { label, prompt }
Chat messages: ChatMessage[] with role:'user' | role:'assistant'
Streaming pipeline: readSSEStream → createJsonlParser → useUITree → UITreeRenderer
Action system: ActionEvent → ActionHandlerMap → ActionResult (discriminated union)
A/B panels: ComparisonPanels with independent useUITree per panel
